content:
M: When I got caught smoking the first time, my dad said ‘grow up’ and sent me to a drug counselor. I mean, every time I go home to Michigan I raid my parents medicine cabinet. So I’m guessing what they say when they mean grow up means start taking grown up drugs.

A: Yeah, weed is for kids. Take the drugs that make us more productive and fit in to capitalist perfection. But honestly, it is so weird how both of those drug families are viewed. I remember showing up to my grandfather’s club when I was probably 18 or 19, drunk, and we smoked in the car right before hand, and I got the intense spins so they thought I was super fucked up on something. I told them I took painkillers instead of copping to smoking weed. I don’t know why that was my first instinct.

M: maybe because that’s what they understand best? Or can relate to? When my dad left my mom for a bit they both got fresh prescriptions of Xanax to cope with it, so there was a time when I was going back and forth between my mom who stayed at our childhood home, and my dad who was staying at my grandparents, and I was popping their Xanax, and it was nice to float and forget everything for awhile. But there was a few days where I don’t really remember anything and that was scary. We were at a wake, and I must’ve been coming out of the fog and then all of the sudden I got this intense pain in my chest, a really crazy irrational urge to tell my mom what was going on. She knew something was up because those days I forgot I was acting funny, wearing two different shoes and shit.
context: From document: Web Sitcom Ideas — chunk 4 (chunk 4/798)
